chunk_summary: This section outlines clinic workflows for linking children with Moderate Acute Malnutrition (MAM) to Anganwadi and community services in India, emphasizing registration, supplementation, and coordinated follow-ups per Mission Poshan guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the clinic workflow for linking MAM cases to Anganwadi and community services.
Learn the role of pediatricians, AWWs, and health staff in managing MAM cases.
Identify key entitlements such as THR and HCM and their delivery mechanisms.
Recognize the importance of aligning clinic reviews with community touchpoints to reduce caregiver burden.
key_facts: Mission Poshan guidelines require MAM beneficiaries to be registered at the nearest Anganwadi center.
Supplementation is delivered through entitlements like Take-Home Rations (THR) and Hot Cooked Meals (HCM) for a minimum of 300 days per year.
The Poshan Tracker includes a migration facility to maintain access for families who temporarily relocate.
Clinic workflow includes issuing a written linkage note with child identifiers, anthropometry, and a clear outpatient plan.
Monthly Village Health, Sanitation, and Nutrition Days (VHSND) are key touchpoints for growth monitoring and referrals.
reference_quote: Mission Poshan scheme guidance specifies that beneficiaries must be registered at the nearest Anganwadi centre and that supplementation is delivered through entitlements such as THR and HCM, typically for a minimum of about 300 days per year. (Reference 1)
